name: evil spirits
shortDescription: In Babylonian mythology, evil spirits were malevolent entities believed to cause illness, misfortune, and chaos in the human world. They were often associated with the underworld and seen as agents of destruction.
description: Babylonian mythology is replete with various types of evil spirits, each with distinct characteristics and malevolent purposes. These spirits were not necessarily gods, but rather supernatural beings often associated with the underworld or the fringes of civilization. They were believed to be responsible for a wide range of afflictions, including physical and mental illnesses, bad luck, and social disruption. The Babylonians employed various rituals, incantations, and amulets to ward off these spirits and protect themselves from their harmful influence. Specific types of evil spirits included the Lilu and Lilitu (male and female demons associated with wind and sexual transgression), the Ekimmu (restless spirits of the unburied dead), and the Asag (a hideous demon causing fever and disease). Belief in these spirits permeated Babylonian society, influencing their medical practices, religious rituals, and daily lives. The constant threat posed by these malevolent entities underscored the importance of maintaining ritual purity and seeking the protection of benevolent deities like Shamash, who was seen as a force against darkness and evil.

sources.title: The Babylonian Legends of the Creation
sources.author: E. A. Wallis Budge

sources.title: Demons and Illness from Antiquity to the Early Middle Ages
sources.author: Alberto Ferreiro
